name: smart-rename
title: Smart Rename
description: Extension which takes selected items from Finder or File Explorer and intelligently renames them to nicer filenames.
author: nocdn

preferences.name: openaiApiKey
preferences.title: OpenAI API Key
preferences.description: Your OpenAI API key used to generate rename suggestions.

preferences.name: openaiModel
preferences.title: OpenAI Model
preferences.description: The OpenAI model ID used for rename suggestions.

preferences.name: renamePrompt
preferences.title: Rename Prompt
preferences.description: Instructions sent to the model for each selected file.
preferences.default: You rename files to clean, human-readable titles. Remove resolution tags, dates, version numbers, download IDs, UUIDs, and random hashes. Remove redundant words like final, copy, and download. Replace dots, underscores, and dashes with spaces. Use Title Case. Return only the new base filename without extension or path.

commands.name: smart-rename
commands.title: Smart Rename
commands.subtitle: Selected Files
commands.description: Rename selected Finder or File Explorer items using GPT-5.5

commands.name: undo-rename
commands.title: Undo Rename
commands.subtitle: Smart Rename
commands.description: Undo the most recent Smart Rename batch

commands.name: redo-rename
commands.title: Redo Rename
commands.subtitle: Smart Rename
commands.description: Redo the most recently undone Smart Rename batch

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
